paper_title: Impatiens glandulifera Study on Pollinator Interaction

methods.description: This method describes the process of sampling plant sites and collecting flower samples to study the interaction of Impatiens glandulifera with native pollinators.

steps.description: The study was carried out in Bristol, UK in ten areas invaded by balsam, and ten adjacent non-invaded control areas with comparable plant communities.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify and select study sites in Bristol, UK for sampling

steps.description: At each site, a point sampling approach was used along a 50 m transect whereby a marker stick was placed every meter along the trail.
steps.searchable_summary: Implement point sampling along a 50 m transect at each site

steps.description: From each plant found within a 30 cm radius of this point, we collected three open flowers.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect three open flowers from plants within 30 cm radius of each sampling point

steps.description: These were placed in small vials with the stem submerged in water for transport to the lab.
steps.searchable_summary: Store collected flowers in vials with water for transport

steps.description: In the lab, stigmas were carefully removed from the flowers with clean tweezers and mounted on a slide with fuchsin jelly.
steps.searchable_summary: Remove stigmas and mount on slides with fuchsin jelly

steps.description: All plant species were classified according to the type of stigma: wet, dry or semi-dry.
steps.searchable_summary: Classify plant species by stigma type

methods.method_name: Statistical Analysis of Plant and Pollen Data
methods.description: This method involves statistical analysis to compare plant species richness, pollen type richness, and plant abundance between invaded and non-invaded sites.

steps.description: To check that our data on pollen transfer were not influenced by variation in plant species richness and abundance among study sites, we tested for differences in plant stigma species richness, pollen type richness and plant abundance (estimated by the number of samples per species) between the invaded and non-invaded sites with a one-way analysis of variance.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform one-way ANOVA to test differences in plant and pollen data between sites
